pattern: For each string, enumerate substrings shortest-first and lexicographically, returning the first that appears nowhere in the other strings.
hints: For arr[i], you need the shortest substring that is not a substring of any arr[j] where j != i; among equal-length winners pick the lexicographically smallest.
Collect all substrings of all strings except arr[i] into a set, then scan arr[i]'s substrings in order of increasing length (then alphabetically) to find the first absent one.
Since strings are at most length 20 and there are at most 100 strings, building a set of all foreign substrings per index is feasible in O(n * L^2) total.

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    arr = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 8)
        s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:6]) for _ in range(length))
        arr.append(s)
    return [arr]
